entries.title: Harbor Welcome
entries.trigger: The Drowned Harbor opening and public board establish successfully.
required_public_facts: Drowned Harbor has emerged at extreme low tide
Exploration begins
The water will return

draft_script.spooky: Welcome to Drowned Harbor. The tide has gone farther than good sense permits. Do explore before it remembers the way back.
draft_script.grim: Welcome to Drowned Harbor. The tide has abandoned the town for now. Explore it before the water returns to collect the streets.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: Welcome to Drowned Harbor. The tide has pulled the town open. Explore before the returning water closes it around you.
draft_script.plain_system: Drowned Harbor has emerged at extreme low tide. Explore the town before the water returns.

performance_notes: Warm invitation with a restrained threat
Do not sound triumphant or immediately hostile
Leave space after the location name

candidate_guidance.human_direction: Elegant host, low-middle register, restrained smile, clear location and time pressure.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Adult low-middle voice, articulate, calm, lightly amused, no accent caricature, no improvisation.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No pirate captain, trailer narrator, demon, constant whisper, celebrity imitation, or false urgency.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Select one canonical take, preserve natural breath, trim only excess silence, and keep a dry unprocessed master.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final script, casting, recording, processing, runtime trigger, or production approval.

entries.title: Low-Tide Board Established
entries.trigger: The initial public routes, landmarks, and distant returning waterline are visible.
required_public_facts: The causeway leads into town
Public routes and landmarks are visible
The returning waterline is distant but advancing
prohibited_implications: All visible routes are equally safe

draft_script.spooky: The causeway offers you to the town. The Bellhouse, Salt Market, lifeboat shed, and lighthouse are all visible. So is the water, although it is keeping its distance for the moment.
draft_script.grim: The causeway leads into town. The Bellhouse, Salt Market, lifeboat shed, and lighthouse are visible. The waterline is distant, and returning.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: The causeway leads into the exposed town. The Bellhouse, Salt Market, lifeboat shed, and lighthouse remain visible. Beyond them, the water is already returning.
draft_script.plain_system: The causeway leads into town. Public landmarks and routes are visible. The distant waterline is returning.

performance_notes: Do not imply hidden route quality

candidate_guidance.human_direction: Point out the public board with patient clarity; the final water phrase carries the only warning weight.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Clear landmark enumeration, moderate pace, restrained final warning, no route inference.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No travel-guide cheer, pirate accent, breathless danger, hidden clue emphasis, or recognizable imitation.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Preserve pauses between landmark groups and align the final sentence with the visible returning-water shot.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final geography narration, timing, casting, recording, or production approval.

entries.title: Bellhouse Ledger Introduced
entries.trigger: The Bellhouse Ledger becomes the active public objective.
required_public_facts: The Ledger records public names and marks
The current public objective is to inspect or operate the Ledger
A record is not guaranteed prophecy
prohibited_implications: The Ledger is always correct about the future
A named seat is secretly Bellmarked

draft_script.spooky: The Bellhouse Ledger has made room for your names. Inspect the record, if only to learn what the town believes it has already heard.
draft_script.grim: The Bellhouse Ledger records public names and marks. Inspect it now. A record is evidence, not a promise of what must happen.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: The Bellhouse Ledger has pressed your public names into its swollen pages. Inspect the record. What is written can still be resisted.
draft_script.plain_system: The Bellhouse Ledger records public names and marks. Inspect or operate it as the current objective. Its record does not guarantee a future result.

performance_notes: Use the plainest Underteller mode for the mechanical sentence
Do not foreshadow a hidden Bellmarked seat

candidate_guidance.human_direction: Ordered and exact; flavor surrounds but does not bury the objective and non-prophecy boundary.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Clear rules-first delivery, moderate pace, precise terms, no hidden-seat emphasis.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No church sermon, prophecy voice, traitor hint, whispered name, or celebrity imitation.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Preserve a clean edit point before the mechanical clarification and retain a dry canonical take.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final Ledger script, rule wording, recording, trigger, or production approval.

entries.title: Unresolved Extra Ring Acknowledged
entries.trigger: The public bell count exceeds the visible active-seat count and an unresolved position is added.
required_public_facts: The bell rang once beyond the visible active-seat count
An unresolved public position now exists
No hidden seat identity is established
prohibited_implications: One specific player caused the ring
A new human or character has joined

draft_script.spooky: One ring more than the visible company. How hospitable. The Bellhouse has opened an unresolved place, but it has not named an occupant.
draft_script.grim: The bell rang once beyond the visible active-seat count. One unresolved public position has been added. No seat has been identified.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: The bell rang for one place beyond the visible company. The Bellhouse has opened an unresolved position. It has not yet given that place a body.
draft_script.plain_system: The bell rang once beyond the visible active-seat count. One unresolved public position was added. No seat identity was revealed.

performance_notes: No suspicious emphasis on any seat

candidate_guidance.human_direction: React to the impossible public count, then become mechanically exact about what it does not prove.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Measured surprise followed by neutral clarification; no seat-directed suspicion.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No jump-scare shout, traitor accusation, whispered player name, death knell, or imitation.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Keep separate edit regions for the atmospheric observation and the public-state clarification.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final extra-ring script, state behavior, recording, or production approval.

entries.title: Lighthouse Council Opens
entries.trigger: The Council stage and all currently legal public lighthouse options are displayed.
required_public_facts: The group must choose among the displayed legal lighthouse options
The choice is not committed until confirmation
No option is presented as secretly correct
prohibited_implications: One direction is the correct ending
Discussion is timed when no public timer exists

draft_script.spooky: The lens is prepared to remember a direction. Consider the choices before you, and confirm only the future you are willing to illuminate.
draft_script.grim: Choose among the displayed lighthouse options. Discuss them freely. Nothing is committed until the group confirms its direction.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: The lens waits to turn the town's memory toward one of the displayed futures. Choose carefully. The mechanism does not commit until you confirm.
draft_script.plain_system: Choose among the displayed legal lighthouse options. The group may discuss them. The choice commits only after confirmation.

candidate_guidance.human_direction: Hold several futures open; the factual confirmation rule receives the clearest emphasis.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Slow articulate Council framing, neutral among options, no countdown or moral hint.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No heroic beacon, villain option, countdown, emotional coercion, pirate delivery, or imitation.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Preserve silence after the line and a clean edit point before the confirmation rule.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final Council script, option design, recording, or production approval.

entries.title: High Water Board Transformation Recap
entries.trigger: The High Water Terror Turn, P0.4 transition cue, and authored silence complete; the transformed public board is visible.
required_public_facts: High Water is active
Public land and water routes changed as shown
Players must use the transformed public board
prohibited_implications: Every closed route is permanently lost

draft_script.spooky: High Water. The streets have traded places with the tide. Use the routes now shown, and do not rely on where the town used to be.
draft_script.grim: High Water is active. Land and water routes have changed as shown. Use the transformed board for all movement and rescue decisions.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: High Water. The returning tide has broken the old paths apart. Use only the routes the transformed board still shows.
draft_script.plain_system: High Water is active. Public land and water routes changed as shown. Use the transformed board for movement and rescue decisions.

performance_notes: Begin after the authored post-turn silence
No shouting over SFX or music

candidate_guidance.human_direction: Intensity comes from certainty and reduced ornament, not loudness.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Firm concise recap, clear land and water distinction, controlled intensity, no shouting.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No tsunami panic, trailer boom voice, automatic defeat, hidden route hint, or imitation.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Align the first phrase after the transition silence and preserve a clean plain-system substitute.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final High Water script, board behavior, recording, synchronization, or production approval.

entries.title: Drowned Archive Opens
entries.trigger: The Drowned Archive becomes publicly accessible and its available public objectives appear.
required_public_facts: The Drowned Archive is publicly accessible
Available public records or objectives are displayed
No displayed interpretation is automatically correct
prohibited_implications: One displayed record is the only truth
The Archive guarantees a good ending

draft_script.spooky: The Drowned Archive is open. Its records have survived the water with several different opinions about what that means. Review the public choices before you.
draft_script.grim: The Drowned Archive is now accessible. Its available public records and objectives are displayed. No interpretation is confirmed until the game resolves it.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: The Drowned Archive has opened under the water. Its swollen records and public objectives are displayed. Read them before the room decides what else to keep.
draft_script.plain_system: The Drowned Archive is accessible. Available public records and objectives are displayed. No interpretation is automatically confirmed as correct.

performance_notes: Leave room for players to read
Do not privilege one public option

candidate_guidance.human_direction: Open the space, then yield to reading; no revelation fanfare.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Clear public access and option statement, restrained curiosity, no correct-answer hint.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No magical library, spoken memories, prophetic certainty, choir, or imitation.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Keep a short opening and a separate mechanical clarification that may be replayed alone.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final Archive script, objective interpretation, recording, or production approval.

entries.title: Last Light Begins
entries.trigger: Last Light becomes active and all currently legal public final options are displayed.
required_public_facts: Last Light is active
Displayed final options are currently legal
An option commits only after confirmation
prohibited_implications: One ending is the good ending
The group is forced to choose immediately without a public timer

draft_script.spooky: Last Light. The Harbor has finally reduced itself to choices small enough to hold in one room. Consider the displayed options, and confirm only when you are ready.
draft_script.grim: Last Light is active. The displayed final options are currently legal. Review their public consequences. Nothing commits until confirmation.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: Last Light. The Harbor has narrowed every surviving future to the options before you. Review the consequences. The final mechanism waits for confirmation.
draft_script.plain_system: Last Light is active. Displayed final options are currently legal. Review their public consequences. The selected option commits only after confirmation.

performance_notes: Rare final authority without volume escalation
Do not emotionally rank the options

candidate_guidance.human_direction: Use stillness and exact stress, especially on displayed options and confirmation.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Low-volume final authority, deliberate pace, no countdown or moral preference.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No final battle voice, victory hint, countdown, choir, shouting, or imitation.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Retain generous post-line silence and a clean mechanical replay segment.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final Last Light script, ending design, recording, or production approval.

entries.title: Harbor Bargain Offered
entries.trigger: A validated Harbor bargain becomes available and its authorized terms are displayed on the correct surface.
required_public_facts: A Harbor bargain is available
The authorized terms are displayed
The offer may be accepted or refused
prohibited_implications: Private terms are spoken over shared speakers

draft_script.spooky: The Harbor is prepared to offer terms. Read what it has placed before you. Acceptance is optional; refusal remains perfectly respectable.
draft_script.grim: A Harbor bargain is available. Review the authorized terms on the displayed surface. You may accept or refuse the offer.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: The Harbor has written an offer in water and record. Review the authorized terms. You may bind the bargain, or refuse it.
draft_script.plain_system: A Harbor bargain is available. Review the authorized terms on the displayed surface. Accept or refuse the offer.

performance_notes: Acceptance and refusal must sound equally legal

candidate_guidance.human_direction: Tempt through precision and patience, not deception or pressure.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Close articulate delivery, neutral between accept and refuse, no unscripted terms.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No demon voice, sales pitch, villain laugh, private cost, threat, or imitation.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Keep the offer framing separate from the legal accept-or-refuse statement.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final bargain terms, private delivery, recording, or production approval.

entries.title: Harbor Debt Collected
entries.trigger: A previously accepted bargain reaches an authorized public collection event and its public consequence commits.
required_public_facts: A previously accepted bargain is resolving
The displayed public consequence commits
No additional unstated cost is applied

draft_script.spooky: The Harbor has remembered an agreement. The displayed public consequence now resolves. It will take exactly what was authorized, and nothing more.
draft_script.grim: A previously accepted Harbor bargain is resolving. Apply the displayed public consequence. No additional cost is added.
draft_script.gore_and_dread: The Harbor has returned for the accepted term. The displayed public consequence now commits. The account takes no more than it was given.
draft_script.plain_system: A previously accepted Harbor bargain is resolving. Apply the displayed public consequence. No additional unstated cost is applied.

candidate_guidance.human_direction: State the consequence as fulfillment of an agreement, not personal revenge.
candidate_guidance.synthetic_direction: Controlled grave delivery, clear public consequence and no-extra-cost statement, no improvisation.
candidate_guidance.negative_direction: No villain laugh, revenge voice, private-term reveal, added threat, shouting, or imitation.
candidate_guidance.human_edit_plan: Preserve a clean rules-first segment and align the atmospheric sentence after the public consequence appears.

entries.approval_boundary: Audition-ready draft family only; no final debt script, bargain mechanics, recording, or production approval.
